    93→  backend?: 'default' | 'claude' | 'gemini' | 'codex' | 'opencode' | 'aider'
    94→}
    95→
    96→
    97→interface StoredData {
    98→  workspace: Workspace
    99→  windowBounds?: WindowBounds
   100→  settings?: Settings
   101→}
   102→
   103→export class SessionStore {
   104→  private configPath: string
   105→  private backupPath: string
   106→  private data: StoredData
   107→
   108→  constructor() {
   109→    const configDir = join(app.getPath('userData'), 'config')
   110→    if (!existsSync(configDir)) {
   111→      mkdirSync(configDir, { recursive: true })
   112→    }
   113→    this.configPath = join(configDir, 'workspace.json')
   114→    this.backupPath = join(configDir, 'workspace.json.backup')
   115→    this.data = this.load()
   116→  }
   117→
   118→  private loadFile(path: string): StoredData | null {
   119→    try {
   120→      if (!existsSync(path)) return null
   121→      const stat = statSync(path)
   122→      if (stat.size === 0) return null
   123→      const content = readFileSync(path, 'utf-8')
   124→      const data = JSON.parse(content)
   125→      if (!data?.workspace) return null
   126→      return data
   127→    } catch (e) {
   128→      console.error(`[SessionStore] Failed to load ${path}:`, e)
   129→      return null
   130→    }
   131→  }
   132→
   133→  private load(): StoredData {
   134→    // Try main file first
   135→    const main = this.loadFile(this.configPath)
   136→    if (main && (main.workspace.projects?.length || 0) > 0) {
   137→      return main
   138→    }
   139→
   140→    // Main file is empty/corrupt/missing — try backup
   141→    const backup = this.loadFile(this.backupPath)
   142→    if (backup && (backup.workspace.projects?.length || 0) > 0) {
   143→      console.log('[SessionStore] Main config empty/corrupt, restored from backup (' +
   144→        backup.workspace.projects.length + ' projects)')
   145→      // Restore backup as main file immediately
   146→      try {
   147→        writeFileSync(this.configPath, JSON.stringify(backup, null, 2))
   148→      } catch (e) {
   149→        console.error('[SessionStore] Failed to restore backup to main:', e)
   150→      }
   151→      return backup
   152→    }
   153→
   154→    // If main loaded but had 0 projects (valid empty state), use it
   155→    if (main) return main
   156→
   157→    return {
   158→      workspace: {
   159→        projects: [],
   160→        openTabs: [],
   161→        activeTabId: null
   162→      }
   163→    }
   164→  }
   165→
   166→  private save(): void {
   167→    try {
   168→      const json = JSON.stringify(this.data, null, 2)
   169→
   170→      // Validate we can parse what we're about to write
   171→      JSON.parse(json)
   172→
   173→      // Back up existing file before overwriting (if it has content)
   174→      if (existsSync(this.configPath)) {
   175→        try {
   176→          const stat = statSync(this.configPath)
   177→          if (stat.size > 0) {
   178→            // Only update backup if existing file is valid and has projects
   179→            const existing = this.loadFile(this.configPath)
   180→            if (existing && (existing.workspace.projects?.length || 0) > 0) {
   181→              writeFileSync(this.backupPath, readFileSync(this.configPath))
   182→            }
   183→          }
   184→        } catch (e) {
   185→          console.error('[SessionStore] Failed to update backup:', e)
   186→        }
   187→      }
   188→
   189→      // Atomic write: write to temp file, then rename
   190→      const tmpPath = this.configPath + '.tmp'
   191→      writeFileSync(tmpPath, json)
   192→
   193→      // Verify the temp file was written completely
   194→      const written = readFileSync(tmpPath, 'utf-8')
   195→      JSON.parse(written) // throws if truncated/corrupt
   196→
   197→      renameSync(tmpPath, this.configPath)
   198→    } catch (e) {
   199→      console.error('[SessionStore] Failed to save workspace:', e)
   200→    }
   201→  }
   202→
   203→  getWorkspace(): Workspace {
   204→    return this.data.workspace
   205→  }
   206→
   207→  // Reload workspace from disk (useful when file was modified externally)
   208→  reloadFromDisk(): void {
   209→    console.log('[SessionStore] Reloading workspace from disk')
   210→    this.data = this.load()
   211→    console.log('[SessionStore] Reloaded, projects:', this.data.workspace?.projects?.length || 0)
   212→  }
   213→
   214→  saveWorkspace(workspace: Workspace): void {
   215→    // Protect against overwriting populated workspace with empty one
   216→    const incomingProjects = workspace?.projects?.length || 0
   217→    const currentProjects = this.data.workspace?.projects?.length || 0
   218→    if (incomingProjects === 0 && currentProjects > 0) {
   219→      console.log('[SessionStore] Rejected empty workspace save - current has', currentProjects, 'projects')
   220→      return
   221→    }
   222→    this.data.workspace = workspace
   223→    this.save()
   224→    syncMetaProjects(workspace)
   225→  }
   226→
   227→  getWindowBounds(): WindowBounds | undefined {
   228→    return this.data.windowBounds
   229→  }
   230→
   231→  saveWindowBounds(bounds: WindowBounds): void {
   232→    this.data.windowBounds = bounds
   233→    this.save()
   234→  }
   235→
   236→  getSettings(): Settings {
   237→    return this.data.settings ?? { defaultProjectDir: '', theme: 'default', backend: 'default' }
   238→  }
   239→
   240→  saveSettings(settings: Settings): void {
   241→    this.data.settings = settings
   242→    this.save()
   243→  }
   244→}
   245→

   335→      return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {

   349→            this.port++

   351→            this.start().then(resolve).catch(reject)

   273→      nonce,
   274→      nonceExpires: expiresAt
   275→    }
   276→
   277→    const protocol = this.useTls ? 'https' : 'http'
   278→    return {
   279→      url: `claude-terminal://${primaryIp}:${this.port}?token=${this.token}`,
   280→      token: this.token,
   281→      port: this.port,
   282→      ips,
   283→      fingerprint,
   284→      formattedFingerprint: getFormattedFingerprint(),
   285→      certFingerprint: this.certFingerprint,
   286→      secure: this.useTls,
   287→      nonce,
   288→      nonceExpires: expiresAt,
   289→      qrData: JSON.stringify(qrPayload)
   290→    }
   291→  }
   292→
   293→  generateNonce(): { nonce: string; expiresAt: number } {
   294→    return createNonce()
   295→  }
   296→
   297→  // File push methods
   298→  sendFileToMobile(filePath: string, message?: string): { success: boolean; fileId?: string; error?: string } {
   299→    return filePushSendFile(filePath, message, this.pendingFiles, this.connectedClients)
   300→  }
   301→
   302→  getPendingFiles(): PendingFile[] {
   303→    return getPendingFilesList(this.pendingFiles)
   304→  }
   305→
   306→  removePendingFile(fileId: string): boolean {
   307→    return filePushRemovePendingFile(fileId, this.pendingFiles)
   308→  }
   309→
   310→  getConnectedClientCount(): number {
   311→    return this.connectedClients.size
   312→  }
   313→
   314→  async start(): Promise<void> {
   315→    try {
   316→      // Get TLS certificate (generates on first run)
   317→      if (this.useTls) {
   318→        log('Initializing TLS certificate...')
   319→        const tlsOptions = await getTlsOptions()
   320→        this.certFingerprint = await getCertificateFingerprint()
   321→        this.server = createHttpsServer(tlsOptions, this.app)
   322→        log(`TLS enabled, cert fingerprint: ${this.certFingerprint.slice(0, 16)}...`)
   323→      } else {
   324→        this.server = createHttpServer(this.app)
   325→        log('TLS disabled, using HTTP')
   326→      }
   327→
   328→      this.setupWebSocket()
   329→      startNonceCleanup()
   330→
   331→      this.rateLimitCleanupInterval = setInterval(() => {
   332→        cleanupEndpointRateLimits()
   333→      }, 2 * 60 * 1000)
   334→
   335→      return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
   336→        this.server!.listen(this.port, '0.0.0.0', () => {
   337→          const protocol = this.useTls ? 'HTTPS' : 'HTTP'
   338→          log(`Started ${protocol} server on port ${this.port}`)
   339→          log(`Token: ${this.token.slice(0, 8)}...`)
   340→          if (this.useTls) {
   341→            log(`Cert fingerprint: ${this.certFingerprint.slice(0, 32)}...`)
   342→          }
   343→          resolve()
   344→        })
   345→
   346→        this.server!.on('error', (err: NodeJS.ErrnoException) => {
   347→          if (err.code === 'EADDRINUSE') {
   348→            log(`Port ${this.port} in use, trying ${this.port + 1}`)
   349→            this.port++
   350→            this.server?.close()
   351→            this.start().then(resolve).catch(reject)
   352→          } else {
   353→            log('Server error', { error: String(err) })
   354→            reject(err)
   355→          }
   356→        })
   357→      })
   358→    } catch (error) {
   359→      log('Failed to start server', { error: String(error) })
   360→      throw error
   361→    }
   362→  }
   363→
   364→  stop(): void {
   365→    stopNonceCleanup()
   366→
   367→    if (this.rateLimitCleanupInterval) {
   368→      clearInterval(this.rateLimitCleanupInterval)
   369→      this.rateLimitCleanupInterval = null
   370→    }
   371→
   372→    if (this.wss) {
   373→      this.wss.close()
   374→      this.wss = null
   375→    }
   376→    if (this.server) {
   377→      this.server.close()
   378→      this.server = null
   379→    }
   380→    log('Stopped')
   381→  }
   382→
   383→  isRunning(): boolean {
   384→    return this.server !== null && this.server.listening
   385→  }
   386→}

   389→export * from './types'

    19→function deriveEncryptionKey(): Buffer {

    22→  const machineId = [

    29→

    10→
    11→// Salt for key derivation (app-specific, not secret)
    12→const TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_SALT = 'claude-terminal-token-v1'
    13→const ENCRYPTION_ALGORITHM = 'aes-256-gcm'
    14→
    15→/**
    16→ * Derive a machine-specific encryption key
    17→ * This makes the encrypted token non-portable to other machines
    18→ */
    19→function deriveEncryptionKey(): Buffer {
    20→  // Combine machine-specific values to create a unique key
    21→  // This prevents the token from being useful if copied to another machine
    22→  const machineId = [
    23→    hostname(),
    24→    userInfo().username,
    25→    homedir(),
    26→    process.platform,
    27→    process.arch
    28→  ].join(':')
    29→
    30→  // Use scrypt to derive a 32-byte key
    31→  return scryptSync(machineId, TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_SALT, 32)
    32→}
    33→
    34→/**
    35→ * Encrypt a token for secure storage
    36→ * Returns base64-encoded encrypted data with IV and auth tag
    37→ */
    38→export function encryptToken(token: string): string {
    39→  const key = deriveEncryptionKey()
    40→  const iv = randomBytes(16)
    41→  const cipher = createCipheriv(ENCRYPTION_ALGORITHM, key, iv)
    42→
    43→  let encrypted = cipher.update(token, 'utf8', 'hex')
    44→  encrypted += cipher.final('hex')
    45→

    47→

    49→  return `${iv.toString('hex')}:${authTag.toString('hex')}:${encrypted}`
    50→}

    55→ */

    68→    const decipher = createDecipheriv(ENCRYPTION_ALGORITHM, key, iv)

    72→    decrypted += decipher.final('utf8')

Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me create a comprehensive report of the bugs found.

## Comprehensive Bug Analysis Report

I've thoroughly analyzed the workspace persistence and settings code in this Electron app. Here are the critical bugs found:

---

### **BUG #1: CRITICAL - Lost Workspace Data on Failed Rename (session-store.ts)**

**Location:** `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/session-store.ts:189-200`

**Issue:** The atomic write operation fails to clean up the temporary file if `renameSync` fails, and the exception is silently swallowed.

**Code:**
```typescript
// Atomic write: write to temp file, then rename
const tmpPath = this.configPath + '.tmp'
writeFileSync(tmpPath, json)

// Verify the temp file was written completely
const written = readFileSync(tmpPath, 'utf-8')
JSON.parse(written) // throws if truncated/corrupt

renameSync(tmpPath, this.configPath)
} catch (e) {
  console.error('[SessionStore] Failed to save workspace:', e)
}
```

**Problems:**
- Line 197: If `renameSync()` fails (e.g., permission denied, filesystem issues), the exception is caught and logged, but the tmp file is never deleted
- This causes stale `.tmp` files to accumulate in the config directory
- More critically, if `renameSync` fails, the old workspace.json remains untouched, but the in-memory `this.data` was already modified (line 222 in `saveWorkspace`)
- Future saves will try to overwrite a now-stale backup file with the modified in-memory state

**Scenario:** Process crashes between `renameSync` failure and next restart → workspace data in memory diverges from disk

---

### **BUG #2: CRITICAL - POST /api/workspace Inconsistent Field Access**

**Location:** `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/mobile-server/routes/workspace.ts:67-89`

**Issue:** The POST endpoint checks for `incoming?.workspace?.projects?.length` but the PUT endpoint checks for `incoming?.projects?.length`, and both endpoints call `saveWorkspace(req.body)` with potentially different structures.

**Code:**
```typescript
// POST handler (line 75)
const incomingProjects = incoming?.workspace?.projects?.length || 0
if (incomingProjects === 0) {
  const current = sessionStore.getWorkspace()
  const currentProjects = current?.workspace?.projects?.length || 0  // BUG: nested workspace access

**Issue:** Symlink creation is not rolled back if batch operation fails

**Code:**
```typescript

### **BUG #9: LOW - writeSecureFile Not Atomic**

**Location:** `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/mobile-security/encryption.ts:85-94`

**Issue:** Token file write to disk is not atomic

**Code:**
```typescript
export function writeSecureFile(filePath: string, content: string): void {
  writeFileSync(filePath, content, 'utf-8')  // Direct write, not atomic

  // Set restrictive file permissions (owner read/write only)
  try {
    chmodSync(filePath, 0o600)  // Race window between write and chmod
  } catch {
    // Ignore chmod errors on Windows
  }
}
```

**Problems:**
- File is world-readable between `writeFileSync` and `chmodSync`
- If process crashes between calls, token file created with default permissions
- Not using temp-file-rename pattern like workspace.json

**Scenario:** Token file written → process crashes before chmod → token file world-readable → security breach

### **BUG #10: LOW - Workspace Data Inconsistency in Memory**

**Location:** `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/session-store.ts:214-224`

**Issue:** `saveWorkspace` modifies in-memory state before persisting, causing issues if persistence fails

**Code:**
```typescript
saveWorkspace(workspace: Workspace): void {
  // Protect against overwriting populated workspace with empty one
  const incomingProjects = workspace?.projects?.length || 0
  const currentProjects = this.data.workspace?.projects?.length || 0
  if (incomingProjects === 0 && currentProjects > 0) {
    console.log('[SessionStore] Rejected empty workspace save...')
    return  // Good - doesn't modify
  }
  this.data.workspace = workspace  // Line 222: Modifies in-memory state
  this.save()  // Then tries to persist (line 223)
  syncMetaProjects(workspace)  // Line 224: Triggers side effects
}
```

**Problem:**
- If `this.save()` fails (and silently catches the error), `this.data.workspace` is already modified
- Next `getWorkspace()` call returns modified data that was never persisted
- In-memory state diverges from disk

**Scenario:** Desktop app calls `saveWorkspace()` → file write fails (disk full) → in-memory workspace modified but not persisted → app shows incorrect state

   152→      timestamp: Date.now()
   153→    })
   154→  } catch (error: any) {
   155→    sendError(res, 500, error.message || 'Failed to get voice settings')
   156→  }
   157→})
   158→
   159→/**
   160→ * POST /api/settings/voice/speak
   161→ * Speak text using TTS
   162→ *
   163→ * Body: { text: string }
   164→ */
   165→router.post('/voice/speak', async (req: Request, res: Response) => {
   166→  try {
   167→    const services = getServices()
   168→
   169→    if (!services.voiceSpeak) {
   170→      return sendError(res, 503, 'Voice service not available')
   171→    }
   172→
   173→    const { text } = req.body
   174→
   175→    if (!text || typeof text !== 'string') {
   176→      return sendError(res, 400, 'text is required and must be a string')
   177→    }
   178→
   179→    // Limit text length to prevent abuse
   180→    if (text.length > 5000) {
   181→      return sendError(res, 400, 'text must be 5000 characters or less')
   182→    }
   183→
   184→    const result = await services.voiceSpeak(text)
   185→
   186→    sendResponse(res, result.success ? 200 : 500, {
   187→      success: result.success,
   188→      data: result.audioData ? { audioData: result.audioData } : undefined,
   189→      error: result.error,
   190→      timestamp: Date.now()
   191→    })
   192→  } catch (error: any) {
   193→    sendError(res, 500, error.message || 'Failed to speak text')
   194→  }
   195→})
   196→
   197→/**
   198→ * POST /api/settings/voice/stop
   199→ * Stop TTS playback
   200→ */
   201→router.post('/voice/stop', async (_req: Request, res: Response) => {
   202→  try {
   203→    const services = getServices()
   204→
   205→    if (!services.voiceStopSpeaking) {
   206→      return sendError(res, 503, 'Voice service not available')
   207→    }
   208→
   209→    const result = await services.voiceStopSpeaking()
   210→
   211→    sendResponse(res, result.success ? 200 : 500, {
   212→      success: result.success,
   213→      timestamp: Date.now()
   214→    })
   215→  } catch (error: any) {
   216→    sendError(res, 500, error.message || 'Failed to stop speaking')
   217→  }
   218→})
   219→
   220→// =============================================================================
   221→// CLI Status Routes
   222→// =============================================================================
   223→
   224→/**
   225→ * GET /api/settings/cli/status
   226→ * Get installation status of all CLI tools
   227→ *
   228→ * Returns: { claude, gemini, codex, opencode, aider }
   229→ */
   230→router.get('/cli/status', async (_req: Request, res: Response) => {
   231→  try {
   232→    const services = getServices()
   233→
   234→    const status: Record<string, any> = {}
   235→
   236→    // Check each CLI tool in parallel
   237→    const checks = await Promise.allSettled([
   238→      services.claudeCheck?.() ?? Promise.resolve({ installed: false }),
   239→      services.geminiCheck?.() ?? Promise.resolve({ installed: false }),
   240→      services.codexCheck?.() ?? Promise.resolve({ installed: false }),
   241→      services.opencodeCheck?.() ?? Promise.resolve({ installed: false }),
   242→      services.aiderCheck?.() ?? Promise.resolve({ installed: false })
   243→    ])
   244→
   245→    const cliNames = ['claude', 'gemini', 'codex', 'opencode', 'aider']
   246→
   247→    checks.forEach((result, index) => {
   248→      if (result.status === 'fulfilled') {
   249→        status[cliNames[index]] = result.value
   250→      } else {
   251→        status[cliNames[index]] = { installed: false, error: result.reason?.message }
   252→      }
   253→    })
   254→
   255→    sendResponse(res, 200, {
   256→      success: true,
   257→      data: status,
   258→      timestamp: Date.now()
   259→    })
   260→  } catch (error: any) {
   261→    sendError(res, 500, error.message || 'Failed to check CLI status')
   262→  }
   263→})
   264→
   265→/**
   266→ * GET /api/settings/cli/:tool
   267→ * Get installation status of a specific CLI tool
   268→ *
   269→ * Params: tool - one of: claude, gemini, codex, opencode, aider
   270→ */
   271→router.get('/cli/:tool', async (req: Request, res: Response) => {
   272→  try {
   273→    const services = getServices()
   274→    const { tool } = req.params
   275→
   276→    const checkFunctions: Record<string, (() => Promise<any>) | undefined> = {
   277→      claude: services.claudeCheck,
   278→      gemini: services.geminiCheck,
   279→      codex: services.codexCheck,
   280→      opencode: services.opencodeCheck,
   281→      aider: services.aiderCheck
   282→    }
   283→
   284→    const checkFn = checkFunctions[tool]
   285→    if (!checkFn) {
   286→      return sendError(res, 400, `Unknown tool: ${tool}. Valid tools: ${Object.keys(checkFunctions).join(', ')}`)
   287→    }
   288→
   289→    const result = await checkFn()
   290→
   291→    sendResponse(res, 200, {
   292→      success: true,
   293→      data: result,
   294→      timestamp: Date.now()
   295→    })
   296→  } catch (error: any) {
   297→    sendError(res, 500, error.message || 'Failed to check CLI status')
   298→  }
   299→})
   300→
   301→export default router
   302→
